description: In September 2015, the EPA revealed Volkswagen installed 'defeat devices' in diesel engines to cheat emissions tests. The scandal led to a massive loss of brand trust, the resignation of the CEO, and billions in fines, but the company eventually pivoted to an EV-first strategy to recover market share.

key_events.description: EPA issues notice of violation regarding defeat devices.
CEO Martin Winterkorn resigns citing shock at the scale of fraud.
Major class-action lawsuits filed in the US and Germany.
VW announces total shift toward electric vehicle manufacturing.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling long-term reputational recovery after a catastrophic corporate integrity failure.
